# s10 sample interactions

# Setup:
#   ./s10 gateway -addr :7079 &
#   ./s10 scheduler -interval 1s -echo &

# Telegram simulated webhook:
#   curl -X POST http://localhost:7079/webhook/telegram \
#     -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
#     -d '{"update_id":1,"message":{"from":{"id":42,"username":"yeding"},
#                                    "chat":{"id":99},"text":"hi bot"}}'
#   {"job_id":1}

# Discord simulated interaction:
#   curl -X POST http://localhost:7079/webhook/discord \
#     -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
#     -d '{"type":2,"channel_id":"987","member":{"user":{"id":"42","username":"yeding"}},
#         "data":{"name":"ask","options":[{"name":"prompt","value":"hello"}]}}'
#   {"job_id":2}

# stderr (gateway+scheduler):
#   [gw] listening on 127.0.0.1:7079
#   [gw] telegram: dry-run | discord: dry-run
#   [sched] interval=1s echo=true
#   [sched] [scheduler] running job 1 (source=telegram)
#   [telegram dry-run] would send to chat=99: echo: hi bot
#   [sched] [scheduler] running job 2 (source=discord)
#   [discord dry-run] would post to channel=987: echo: hello

# ./s10 jobs (after both webhooks):
#   #1    telegram  done     {"channel_id":"99","user_id":"yeding#42","text":"hi bot"}
#         result: echo: hi bot
#   #2    discord   done     {"channel_id":"987","user_id":"yeding#42","text":"hello"}
#         result: echo: hello
